text.title: Agent Reasoning Depth Budget Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-reasoning-depth-budget enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Start with the smallest budget the provider allows (or 1024 tokens); double until eval-score gain ≤2% per doubling; stop.
rule.rationale: Plateau detection prevents paying for thinking that no longer moves the score; exponential sweep finds the knee in ≤6 runs.

rule.statement: Record budget × score × cost for at least 4 budgets; pick the smallest budget within 2% of the best score (Pareto pick).
rule.rationale: Best-score pick over-spends; Pareto pick captures 95% of value at ~50% of cost.

rule.statement: Under-thinking detector: ≥30% of failures come from reasoning that stopped before a known sub-step. Over-thinking: judge-score plateau ≥1024 tokens before final answer.
rule.rationale: Two detectors, two fixes: under → raise budget on those task types; over → compress or distill.

rule.statement: Inter-turn compression: at turn N>2, prior thinking traces are summarized to ≤256 tokens before being re-included.
rule.rationale: Without compression, multi-turn agents waste their context on stale reasoning at turn 5+.

rule.statement: Re-evaluate budget after every model version upgrade; old budgets may over-pay or under-think on the new model.
rule.rationale: Reasoning efficiency changes between model versions; stale budgets degrade quality silently.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
